title: OpenClaw OpenShell FS bridge read was TOCTOU-racy, letting a symlink swap read outside the sandbox mount root
description: OpenShellFsBridge.readFile() in extensions/openshell/src/fs-bridge.ts (lines 45-59) validates hostPath with assertLocalPathSafety() and then, as a separate operation, calls fsPromises.readFile(hostPath), re-resolving the path through the live filesystem. Because the boundary check (time-of-check) and the read-by-path (time-of-use) are not atomic, an attacker who swaps a parent directory (or the leaf) for a symlink between the check and the read can cause readFile to follow the swapped link and return bytes from a file outside target.mountHostRoot. This is a TOCTOU (CWE-367) symlink-swap race that escapes the sandbox mount-root pinning for reads.
